hints: Read the attacker's input the way the database does. The leading single quote closes the empty username string; then OR '1'='1' is a condition that is true for every row.
The trailing -- turns the rest of the line into a comment, so the whole AND password = '...' check disappears. What survives is just: WHERE username = '' OR '1'='1'.
Replace the WHERE clause with  WHERE username = '' OR '1'='1'  and press Run. Every account comes back -- no password required.
